title: RSI — Full Stomach / Aspiration Risk
description: An obese adult with GERD and a full stomach requires rapid sequence induction without mask ventilation before first laryngoscopy.
learningObjectives: Sustain three minutes of 100% oxygen preoxygenation
Apply cricoid pressure independently and avoid PPV before first laryngoscopy
Confirm placement with sustained continuous EtCO2 before releasing cricoid pressure

debrief.summary: This case scores the RSI sequence, including three-minute preoxygenation, independent cricoid pressure, no pre-laryngoscopy PPV, timed intubation, sustained EtCO2 confirmation, and transition to mechanical ventilation.
teachingPoints: Obesity reduces FRC through the existing body-geometry oxygen-store model.
Cricoid pressure is recorded independently of bag-mask ventilation.
Continuous EtCO2 confirmation must be sustained before cricoid release.
